text.title: Python Async Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Python Async Patterns methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Async code paths MUST use async drivers (httpx, asyncpg, aioboto3); sync calls (requests, psycopg2, time.sleep) on the hot path are forbidden and flagged by lint.
rule.rationale: A blocking sync call freezes the event loop and stalls all in-flight requests on the worker.

rule.statement: Every `await` on a network call MUST be wrapped in `asyncio.timeout(...)` (3.11+) or an explicit per-call timeout argument; bare awaits on the wire are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without a timeout, a hung upstream blocks the task forever, exhausting worker concurrency.

rule.statement: Fan-out parallelism MUST be bounded by `asyncio.Semaphore` or `TaskGroup` with explicit cap; raw `asyncio.gather(*N_unknown)` is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Unbounded gather over N=10000 tasks creates 10000 sockets and futures simultaneously, exhausting fd / RAM.

rule.statement: Multi-task fan-out MUST use `asyncio.TaskGroup` (3.11+) for structured concurrency; loose `asyncio.create_task` without retention is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Loose create_task lets exceptions vanish silently and tasks outlive their parent; TaskGroup propagates failures and bounds lifetime.

rule.statement: Exception handlers MUST re-raise `asyncio.CancelledError`; bare `except:` or `except Exception:` that swallows cancellation is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Swallowing CancelledError makes shutdown hang and breaks TaskGroup cleanup.

rule.statement: Mandatory sync work (compression, legacy driver) MUST run via `asyncio.to_thread` or a documented executor pool; inline sync calls are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Explicit offload makes the boundary visible; inline sync silently re-blocks the loop.

rule.statement: Each worker process MUST run exactly one event loop; mixing `asyncio.run` calls inside running loops is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Nested loops raise RuntimeError at runtime and indicate a flawed architectural split.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if all entries in `## Applies If` hold and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec - decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: A misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch from the decision tree.
